---
title: "Data Handling"
subtitle: "Filter rows, pick columns, sort, and reshape your data with dplyr."
toc: true
---

Run this setup chunk first — it creates the dataset used in all examples below.

```{webr-r}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

capsules <- tibble(
  capsule_id   = 1:10,
  batch        = c("A","A","A","A","A","B","B","B","B","B"),
  fill_mass_mg = c(174.2, 174.4, 174.7, 174.0, 174.5,
                   175.1, 174.8, 175.3, 174.6, 175.0),
  pct_label    = c(99.8, 100.1, 100.3, 99.6, 100.0,
                   100.5, 100.2, 100.7, 99.9, 100.4)
)

capsules
```

## filter( )

Keep only rows where a condition is `TRUE`.

```{webr-r}
# Keep only batch A capsules
capsules %>%
  filter(batch == "A")
```

```{webr-r}
# Keep rows matching two conditions
capsules %>%
  filter(batch == "B" & pct_label > 100)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Comparison operators
`==` equal · `!=` not equal · `>` `<` `>=` `<=` greater/less than · `&` and · `|` or
:::

## select( )

Keep only the columns you need.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  select(capsule_id, batch, pct_label)
```

```{webr-r}
# Drop a column with -
capsules %>%
  select(-fill_mass_mg)
```

## rename( )

Rename a column: `rename(new_name = old_name)`.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  rename(id = capsule_id, pct = pct_label)
```

## arrange( )

Sort rows by a column. Wrap in `desc()` to sort highest-first.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_label))
```

## mutate( )

Add or transform columns. See the full [`mutate()` reference page](mutate.qmd) for details.

```{webr-r}
label_claim_mg <- 75.0

capsules %>%
  mutate(ibu_mg = fill_mass_mg * 0.43) %>%
  select(capsule_id, fill_mass_mg, ibu_mg, pct_label)
```

## Combining steps

Chain any number of operations with the pipe — each result flows into the next.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  filter(batch == "B") %>%
  arrange(pct_label) %>%
  select(capsule_id, fill_mass_mg, pct_label)
```
